title: Workplace Harassment (POSH Act)
description: Protection against sexual harassment at workplace under the POSH Act 2013
your_rights: Right to file a complaint with the Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) within 3 months of incident
Right to confidentiality during inquiry — your identity cannot be disclosed
Right to be transferred or get paid leave during inquiry without loss of benefits
Right to appeal if unsatisfied with ICC decision within 90 days
Right to compensation covering lost earnings, medical expenses, and mental anguish
Employer is legally obligated to constitute an ICC if workforce exceeds 10 employees
steps_to_take: Document the incident with exact dates, times, locations, and any witnesses
Report to Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) of your organization in writing
If no ICC exists, report to Local Complaints Committee (LCC) of your district
File police complaint under IPC Section 354A for physical harassment
Approach National Commission for Women if complaint is not addressed

title: Rights During Police Arrest
description: Fundamental rights every citizen has when arrested by police under Article 22 of the Constitution
your_rights: Right to know the reason for arrest (Article 22) — police must tell you why
Right to inform a family member or friend about your arrest immediately
Right to meet a lawyer of your choice immediately after arrest
Right to be produced before a magistrate within 24 hours of arrest
Right to remain silent — you cannot be forced to confess under Article 20(3)
Right to medical examination if you claim torture or injury in custody
Women cannot be arrested after sunset or before sunrise except in exceptional cases with female officer present
Right to free legal aid if you cannot afford a lawyer under Article 39A
Right to bail in bailable offences — police cannot deny it
steps_to_take: Calmly ask the officer the reason for arrest and demand it in writing
Do not resist physically — note badge number and name of arresting officer
Immediately call a family member or lawyer — you have this right
Do not sign any document without reading fully or without a lawyer present
Remember or note the police station name and FIR number
If tortured in custody, report to magistrate during production within 24 hours

title: Filing an FIR
description: How to file a First Information Report (FIR) with the police
your_rights: Police CANNOT refuse to register an FIR for a cognizable offence — it is a legal obligation
You can file FIR at any police station regardless of jurisdiction (Zero FIR)
You are entitled to a free copy of the FIR immediately after registration
If police refuse, you can send written complaint to Superintendent of Police by post
You can also file FIR online on state police portals
Magistrate can order police to register FIR if they refuse (Section 156(3) CrPC)
steps_to_take: Go to the nearest police station and meet the officer in charge
Give a written or oral complaint — ensure all facts, dates, and names are clearly mentioned
Collect the FIR copy with FIR number — it is free and your legal right
If refused, write to DSP/SP or file complaint on state police website
If still refused, file application before Magistrate under Section 156(3) CrPC
For women related crimes, complaint can be given to female officer only
helplines: Online FIR: check your state police website

title: Cybercrime & Online Fraud
description: What to do if you are a victim of online fraud, scam, hacking, or cybercrime under IT Act 2000
your_rights: Right to report cybercrime under IT Act 2000 and IPC
Right to get money back if bank fraud is reported within 24 hours (RBI circular)
Right to file FIR at any cyber crime cell regardless of where fraud occurred
Right to remain anonymous when reporting online on cybercrime.gov.in
Right to compensation from fraudsters if convicted
Social media platforms must remove illegal content within 36 hours of complaint
steps_to_take: For financial fraud: immediately call your bank helpline to block the transaction
Call 1930 (National Cyber Crime Helpline) within first hour for best chance of recovery
Report on cybercrime.gov.in — keep the complaint number for follow-up
Take screenshots of all evidence — messages, payment confirmations, sender details
File FIR at nearest cyber crime cell or police station
For fake job offers: report on Sanchar Saathi portal (sancharsaathi.gov.in)
For online harassment or morphed images: report on cybercrime.gov.in under women/child section
helplines: Bank fraud: call your bank immediately

title: Consumer Rights & Product Complaints
description: Rights when you buy a defective product or receive poor service under Consumer Protection Act 2019
your_rights: Right to get refund, replacement, or repair for defective products
Right to file complaint in Consumer Court without a lawyer — self-representation allowed
Right to compensation for mental agony, legal costs, and losses caused
Right to claim within 2 years of the defect being noticed
E-commerce and online purchases are fully covered under Consumer Protection Act 2019
Right to product liability — manufacturer is responsible for harm caused by defective product
Right to fair contract — unfair terms in agreements can be challenged
steps_to_take: First raise complaint directly with the seller or company in writing — keep the email/chat
If unresolved within 30 days, file complaint on consumerhelpline.gov.in
Call National Consumer Helpline: 1800-11-4000 (toll free, Mon-Sat)
For disputes up to Rs 50 lakh: file in District Consumer Commission (no lawyer needed)
For Rs 50 lakh to 2 crore: State Consumer Commission
Keep all bills, receipts, warranty cards, and communication as evidence
For e-commerce: also file complaint on the platform's grievance portal first

title: Tenant & Rental Rights
description: Legal rights of tenants against landlords in India
your_rights: Landlord cannot evict you without proper legal notice — minimum 15 days as per agreement
Landlord cannot forcibly enter your rented premises without prior permission
Security deposit must be returned within reasonable time after vacating (usually 30 days)
Rent cannot be increased without proper notice and as per rental agreement terms
Right to basic amenities — landlord cannot cut water, electricity to force eviction
Tenant cannot be harassed or threatened — it is an offence under IPC
Under Model Tenancy Act 2021: security deposit capped at 2 months rent for residential
steps_to_take: Always have a written registered rental agreement — verbal agreements are hard to prove
Document property condition with photos and video when moving in and moving out
If illegally evicted or threatened: file complaint at local police station immediately
For security deposit issues: file case in Rent Control Court or District Consumer Forum
Approach Rent Authority of your city for official disputes
If landlord cuts utilities: file complaint in civil court for injunction

title: Domestic Violence
description: Legal protection against domestic violence under Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005
your_rights: Right to live in the shared household — cannot be forced out even if you don't own it
Right to a Protection Order against the abuser issued by Magistrate
Right to monetary relief for medical expenses, loss of income, and maintenance
Right to custody of children in emergency situations
Right to free legal services under Legal Services Authorities Act
Physical, emotional, verbal, economic, and sexual abuse are all covered under this Act
Male victims, children, and elderly can also seek protection under IPC Sections 323, 324, 498A
steps_to_take: If in immediate danger: call Police 100 or Women Helpline 181 RIGHT NOW
Contact a Protection Officer in your district — they are available free of charge
File complaint at nearest police station under IPC Section 498A for cruelty
Approach Magistrate Court directly for emergency Protection Order
Contact an NGO or shelter home for immediate safety and legal support
Collect evidence: photos of injuries, medical reports, witness contacts, threatening messages

title: Road Accident Rights
description: Legal rights and compensation process after a road accident under Motor Vehicles Act 2019
your_rights: Right to free first aid and emergency treatment at any hospital — cannot be denied for lack of police clearance
Right to compensation from third-party insurance of the at-fault vehicle
Good Samaritans who help accident victims are legally protected from police harassment under MVA 2019
Hit-and-run victims can claim compensation from Solatium Fund (Rs 25,000 for grievous injury, Rs 2 lakh for death)
Right to claim within 6 months for hit-and-run from MACT
Right to interim compensation within 30 days of filing MACT claim
steps_to_take: Call 112 for ambulance and police immediately — do not move the injured unnecessarily
Take photos/video of accident scene, vehicle positions, number plates, and injuries
Get a copy of the FIR and Medico-Legal Certificate (MLC) from the hospital
Collect contact details of witnesses at the scene
File insurance claim with the at-fault vehicle's insurance company within 30 days
For compensation: file petition at Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) in your district
Hire a lawyer for MACT proceedings — many work on contingency for accident cases
helplines: Motor Accident Claims: contact district MACT court

title: Right to Information (RTI)
description: How to use RTI Act 2005 to get information from any government office
your_rights: Every Indian citizen can request information from any government department or public authority
Response must be given within 30 days (48 hours for matters involving life or liberty)
RTI fee is only Rs 10 — BPL citizens are completely exempt from all fees
You do not need to give any reason for asking information
Right to first appeal within 30 days if information is denied or incomplete
Right to second appeal to Central/State Information Commission if first appeal fails
Right to compensation if information is wrongly denied (Rs 250/day up to Rs 25,000)
steps_to_take: Write a simple application to the Public Information Officer (PIO) of the concerned department
Clearly state: what information you need, which period, which office
Pay Rs 10 fee via postal order, bank draft, or online at rtionline.gov.in
Submit physically or file online at rtionline.gov.in for central government departments
For state departments: use the respective state RTI portal
If denied or no response in 30 days: file First Appeal to the Appellate Authority
If First Appeal fails: file Second Appeal to Central/State Information Commission

title: Worker & Labour Rights
description: Basic rights of employees and workers under Indian labour law
your_rights: Right to minimum wage as per state government rates — varies by state and industry
Right to 12 days paid leave per year under Factories Act (earned leave)
Right to overtime pay at double the regular rate for hours beyond 9 per day or 48 per week
Right to Provident Fund (PF) contribution from employer if salary is below Rs 15,000
Right to safe and healthy working conditions under Factories Act
Right to gratuity after 5 years of continuous service (15 days salary per year worked)
Permanent employees cannot be terminated without valid reason and proper notice
Right to equal pay for equal work regardless of gender
steps_to_take: For wage theft or minimum wage violation: file complaint at Labour Commissioner office
For PF issues or employer not depositing PF: file grievance at epfindia.gov.in or call 1800-118-005
For wrongful termination: approach Labour Court with employment documents
For unsafe conditions: report to Factory Inspector of your district
For gratuity non-payment: file complaint with Controlling Authority (Labour Commissioner)
Keep copies of offer letter, salary slips, PF statements, and all work communication
For ESIC (medical benefit) issues: contact nearest ESIC office or esic.gov.in

title: Land & Property Rights
description: Rights of citizens regarding land ownership, disputes, and property fraud
your_rights: Right to inspect land records (khata, patta, mutation) at the sub-registrar office
Right to get certified copies of sale deed and property documents
Right to file complaint if property is fraudulently sold without your consent
Benami property transactions are illegal under Benami Transactions Act 1988
Right to approach civil court for property disputes and partition suits
Right to get property mutated in your name after purchase at tehsil/revenue office
steps_to_take: Verify land records online through your state's land records portal (e.g., Bhoomi in Karnataka, Bhulekh in UP)
For fraud or forged documents: file FIR at police station and complaint at Sub-Registrar office
For property disputes between family members: file partition suit in civil court
For illegal encroachment on your land: file complaint at local tehsildar office
Get property mutation done immediately after purchase to establish legal ownership
For benami property complaints: report to Income Tax Department
helplines: Revenue Helpline: contact your state revenue department

title: Education Rights
description: Rights of students and parents under education law in India
your_rights: Right to free and compulsory education for children aged 6-14 under RTE Act 2009
Right to 25% reserved seats in private schools for economically weaker sections (EWS) under RTE
No child can be expelled or failed until Class 8 under RTE Act
Right to get mark sheets, certificates, and migration certificates from school/college
University/college cannot withhold degree certificate for pending dues (Supreme Court ruling)
Right to re-evaluation or re-checking of answer sheets in most universities
Students with disabilities have right to accessibility and reasonable accommodation
steps_to_take: For RTE admission (EWS): apply at your district education office before deadline
If school denies TC or certificates: file complaint at District Education Officer (DEO)
If college withholds degree: approach University Grievance Cell or file writ petition in High Court
For ragging complaints: file on antiragging.in or call 1800-180-5522 (toll free)
For unfair exam results: apply for re-evaluation within the deadline given by university
For fee hike disputes: approach state Fee Regulatory Committee
helplines: RTE helpline: contact district education office

title: Disability Rights
description: Rights of persons with disabilities under Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act 2016
your_rights: Right to free education in government schools up to age 18
Right to 4% reservation in government jobs for persons with benchmark disabilities
Right to 5% reservation in government higher education institutions
Right to Unique Disability ID (UDID) card for accessing all benefits
Right to accessibility in public buildings, transport, and digital platforms
Right to equal treatment and protection from discrimination
Right to legal capacity — persons with disabilities can enter contracts, vote, and own property
steps_to_take: Get Disability Certificate from Chief Medical Officer (CMO) of your district
Apply for UDID card at swavlambancard.gov.in for unified access to all schemes
For job reservation: apply through respective state/central government job portals
For discrimination complaints: file with Chief Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities (CCPD)
For accessibility violations in public places: file complaint with district administration
For education benefits: approach DEO or university disability cell
